meta.title: Antigravity Dev Session Log — Haazir AI Orchestrator

session_log.message: update logo and colors of the app. light mode. do it as efficiently as possible without using much tokens but do it properly without any mistakes. App name is 'Haazir' now.
Renaming app from Khedmatgar → Haazir and switching to full light mode. Here's my plan before I touch anything.

plan.title: Haazir Rebrand + Light Mode Implementation Plan
steps: 1. Create SVG logo asset (haazir_logo.svg) with brand green paths
2. Add flutter_svg dependency to pubspec.yaml
3. Update app name in pubspec.yaml, AndroidManifest.xml
4. Rewrite ThemeData in main.dart — light mode, AppBarTheme, BottomNavTheme
5. Replace all screen AppBars: backgroundColor #163300, SVG logo title
6. Remove all LinearGradient usages (9 occurrences across 5 files)
7. Replace Colors.amber with Color(0xFF3A9010) on provider side
8. Update widget_test.dart — old class name reference

session_log.message: use the same logo on the provider side as well
Added SvgPicture.asset('assets/haazir_logo.svg', height: 26) to all provider screen AppBars. All 11 screens now use the same logo.
keep the colors same for both customer and provider
Replaced all Colors.amber with const Color(0xFF3A9010) across all provider screens. No more divergence between customer and provider palettes.
session_log.changes: grep replaced 14 instances of Colors.amber across provider screens

session_log.message: is it possible for you to export anti gravity trace logs showing language parsing confidence, provider ranking rationale, scheduling decisions, price logic, action execution, and fallback behavior?
Yes — best approach is per-session JSON files written by each agent as it runs. Here's the architecture.

plan.title: Agent Trace Logging Architecture
plan.approach: Each agent calls logTraceEvent(session_id, payload) which appends to backend/data/agent_traces/<session_id>.json
agents_to_instrument: IntentParser — confidence score, clarification decision, language detected
ProviderMatcher — eligible pool, scoring breakdown per provider, fallback flag
PricingEngine — quote breakdown, surge flag, fairness note
BookingSimulator — booking_id, scheduled time, collision flag
StatusMachine — every status transition with from/to
RatingEngine — stars, on_time flag, rating delta
new_endpoints: GET /api/trace/session/:session_id — full trace for one session
GET /api/trace/latest — most recently modified trace file
plan.session_id_linkage: session_id stored on booking record so any downstream agent can look it up without extra params

session_log.message: i guess json works. do the things. but don't export the logs yet. i'll do the flow in the app and then you do it
Instrumented all agents. Trace infrastructure live.

files_modified: backend/src/routes/routes.ts — DisputeAgent hook + 2 new trace endpoints
key_decisions: session_id stored on booking record rather than threaded through every function call — simpler
Trace files are append-only JSON arrays — no DB needed
logTraceEvent is fire-and-forget — does not block agent execution

session_log.message: bro colors are not accessible on light mode. what did you do?? i see white text on white backgrounds. you even changed the logo text color. redo colors. logo needs to be consistent on ALL SCREENS. THE SAME LOGO. fix all contrast issues. make sure the app colors are accessible in every way. don't use gradients anywhere across the entire app
Full accessibility audit complete. Identified 9 gradient usages, multiple white-on-white text instances, and logo inconsistencies. Here is the complete fix plan.

plan.title: WCAG 2.2 AA Accessibility Fix Plan

contrast_ratios.#163300_on_white: 13.6:1 — use for primary text on light bg
contrast_ratios.#3A9010_on_white: 3.77:1 — passes UI threshold (3:1) but FAILS text (4.5:1) — icons/borders only
contrast_ratios.#B0B5AE_on_white: 2.04:1 — FAILS both thresholds — decorative borders only
contrast_ratios.#9FE870_on_#163300: 9.4:1 — logo paths on dark AppBar — fine

issues_found: jobs_screen.dart AppBar backgroundColor: Colors.white — white TabBar labels on white AppBar (invisible)
bookings_screen.dart — 5x Colors.white.withValues(alpha:X) as text on white card backgrounds
jobs_screen.dart — 4x Colors.white.withValues(alpha:X) as text on white card backgrounds
customer/chat_screen.dart — user bubble Colors.black text on Color(0xFF163300) dark bg (1.34:1 — fails)
customer/chat_screen.dart — AI bubble Color(0xFFE2E8F0) near-white text on white bg (fails)
chat_widgets.dart — Color(0xFFE2E8F0) in 3 places on white/light backgrounds
chat_widgets.dart — Colors.amber as text on white bg (1.78:1 — fails both thresholds)
provider_dashboard_screen.dart — Colors.white text on white card
alerts_screen.dart — Colors.amber icon on white bg
customer_home.dart — amber To Provider button on dark AppBar

fix_strategy.gradients: Remove all 9 LinearGradient usages — replace with flat colors appropriate to context
fix_strategy.white_on_white: Replace Colors.white.withValues(alpha:X) on light/white bgs with Color(0xFF767773), Color(0xFF21231D), or Color(0xFF3A9010) as appropriate
fix_strategy.amber_as_text: Replace Colors.amber text with Color(0xFF7A5400) — dark amber, ~7:1 on white
fix_strategy.near_white_text: Replace Color(0xFFE2E8F0) with Color(0xFF3E3F3B) on all light backgrounds
fix_strategy.appbar_consistency: All AppBars set to Color(0xFF163300) with white icons and SVG logo

session_log.message: on the provider screen you're using white tabs on white background??? Can you please fix all color issues in a single go??

Root cause found: jobs_screen.dart line 93 had backgroundColor: Colors.white on the AppBar — TabBar labels were Color(#FFFFFF) on a white AppBar = completely invisible. Fixed across all files in one pass.

items.fix: AppBar bg Colors.white → #163300 (CRITICAL — root cause of white tabs)
5x white.withValues(alpha) on card text → proper dark colors
User bubble text Colors.black on #163300 → Colors.white
To Provider button amber → white (on dark AppBar)
Jobs Completed value Colors.white on white card → #21231D
To Provider button amber → white on dark AppBar

session_log.result: flutter analyze — 0 errors. No remaining LinearGradient, no white-on-white text, no amber text on light backgrounds.

session_log.message: we'll deploy this new apk as well later. but we need to deploy the backend as well so that anyone can use this apk then. how would we do that?
Two things need to change in code, then deploy backend to Railway or Render.

walkthrough.title: Backend Deployment Walkthrough
code_changes_needed: backend/src/index.ts — already uses process.env.PORT || 3000 and cors() wide open — no changes needed
frontend/lib/services/api_service.dart — base URL hardcoded to localhost — needs production URL
walkthrough.approach: Use --dart-define=BACKEND_URL=https://... at build time — clean, no code change per deploy
walkthrough.api_service_change: Added const _prodUrl = String.fromEnvironment('BACKEND_URL') — falls back to emulator URL for dev builds
walkthrough.gotcha: Backend uses flat JSON files for storage — ephemeral on cloud. Data resets on restart. Acceptable for demo.

session_log.message: make the changes, then also build the apk and tell me what i need to do manually
Code changes made. APK built. Here's exactly what you need to do manually.
changes_made: backend/src/index.ts — updated service name to Haazir
frontend/lib/services/api_service.dart — refactored baseUrl to use --dart-define pattern
.gitignore — added backend/dist (Railway needs to build TS itself, not use committed artifacts)

apk_build_result.note: Java was missing from PATH — found at /opt/homebrew/Cellar/openjdk@17/17.0.19, used directly

manual_steps: 2. Railway: New Project → Deploy from GitHub → set Root Directory = backend → add GOOGLE_API_KEY env var
OPTIONAL: Add JAVA_HOME to ~/.zshrc so you don't need the prefix each time

session_log.message: [shared screenshot] Railway showing failed to fetch when selecting repo
GitHub App permissions issue. Fix: Configure GitHub App → Repository Access → ensure ai-service-orchestrator is listed → Refresh.

walkthrough.title: Railway GitHub App Fix
steps: Click Configure GitHub App in Railway UI
GitHub opens → scroll to Repository Access
Add ai-service-orchestrator to allowed repos (or select All)
Save → back to Railway → Refresh
walkthrough.fallback: If still broken: New Project → Empty Project → + New Service → GitHub Repo (alternative auth flow)

session_log.message: Railway seems to be down. Help me deploy somewhere else. Free.
Render is the best free alternative. Clear UI, auto-detects Node.js, has a Root Directory field.

walkthrough.title: Render Deployment Walkthrough
steps: Sign up with GitHub
New → Web Service
Connect munirhassan-ux/ai-service-orchestrator
Root Directory: backend
Build Command: npm install && npm run build
Start Command: npm start
Instance Type: Free
Environment Variables: GOOGLE_API_KEY = <key>
Click Deploy Web Service
walkthrough.caveat: Free tier spins down after 15min inactivity — 50s cold start. Fine for demo.

session_log.message: Render asks for card even on free tier
Render added card verification recently even for free tier. The $1 hold drops in a few days — you won't be charged. Safe to add card if staying on Free instance type.
[screenshot] Render build live — https://haazir-backend-fxub.onrender.com
Backend is live. Building final APK with production URL baked in.
can i now upload the app-release.apk file anywhere and share and it'll work?
Yes. Share via Google Drive, Telegram, or WeTransfer. Recipients need to enable Install from Unknown Sources on Android.

walkthrough.title: APK Distribution Guide
share_options: Google Drive / Dropbox — upload, get link, share
Telegram — send file directly in chat (WhatsApp sometimes blocks APKs)
WeTransfer — no account, link lasts 7 days
recipient_steps: Android: For your security... → Settings → Enable Install from Unknown Sources

session_log.message: stage, commit, push to branch-saad and merge with origin-main and return to branch-saad

All code changes already committed in bd54056. Only outstanding item was a tracked .dart_tool lock file that should be gitignored. Removed from tracking, committed, pushed, merged to main, returned to branch-saad.
git_operations: git commit -m 'remove tracked build artifact from git'
git checkout main && git merge branch-saad (fast-forward)
session_log.final_state: Both branch-saad and main at dbefd40. Working branch: branch-saad.

session_log.message: i forgot to ask you to export trace/logs. can you automate the flow and just create a mock one for me? make it so those look like antigravity chat logs

Found 42 real trace files from actual sessions. Best complete trace: SESS-1779295876575-23 (full end-to-end including StatusMachine + RatingEngine). Best fallback showcase: SESS-1779028791327-19 (2x provider timeouts, warm restarts). Combined both into haazir_trace_export.json — comprehensive showcase covering all 7 agents and all fallback paths.

trace_export.scenario: Roman Urdu AC repair request → 2 clarification turns → provider timeout + warm restart → full status lifecycle → 5-star rating
highlights: IntentParser: confidence 0.9 → 0.2 → 0.95 across 3 turns, language switches english → roman_urdu mid-session
ProviderMatcher: 5 candidates scored on 8 weighted factors, full breakdown per provider
PricingEngine: all 3 providers priced, budget pick flagged, urgency surcharge applied on warm restart
Orchestrator: provider 1 times out (180s), warm restart count incremented, excluded_providers list maintained
BookingSimulator: scheduling collision detected and resolved
StatusMachine: full 5-state lifecycle PENDING → ACCEPTED → ARRIVING → ARRIVED → IN_PROGRESS → COMPLETED
RatingEngine: 5 stars, rating delta 4.6 → 4.61 applied to provider profile

major_milestones: App rebranded from Khedmatgar to Haazir with new SVG logo and full light mode
Agent trace logging infrastructure built — 7 agents instrumented, 2 new API endpoints
Comprehensive WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility audit — 20+ contrast violations fixed
Production APK built with Render URL baked in via --dart-define (51.9MB)
Full session trace exported as haazir_trace_export.json
key_technical_decisions: session_id stored on booking record — avoids threading it through every function call
Trace files are append-only JSON arrays — no database needed for demo
--dart-define pattern for APK URL injection — no code changes between dev and prod builds
Java not on PATH — resolved using JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/Cellar/openjdk@17/17.0.19 prefix
Colors.amber as text fails WCAG (1.78:1) — replaced with Color(0xFF7A5400) dark amber (~7:1)
